rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.note: Spike No More addresses spike-driven instability via static sub-layer/shortcut ratio, not per-layer learnable coefficient nor spike-triggered dampening; candidate explicitly differentiates from it.

GPAS uses per-layer learnable SiLU-gate scalar on activation post-residual with stop-gradient; preserves gradient magnitude rather than dampening based on spike detection — candidate is gradient-scaling not activation-scaling and is spike-triggered.
Highway-style residual is a static architectural change for gradient flow, not a learnable spike-triggered depth-graded dampener.
Peri-LN is about LayerNorm placement; no spike-trigger, no learnable gradient-propagation scaling coefficient.
Per-layer gradient noise prediction for noise scale estimation — observational, not an intervention dampening propagation.
Standard global clip-by-norm and adaptive clipping; not per-layer, not depth-graded, not spike-triggered learnable scaling.
Depth-aware re-architecting (deeper layers under-utilised) — diagnostic/architectural, not learnable spike-triggered gradient dampener.
N:M sparsity-induced gradient noise; concerns sparsity training, not spike-triggered per-layer dampening of gradient propagation.

fail_reason: No prior art result meets keyword, semantic, or functional thresholds — the QAJAQ-DAMPER combination of (1) per-layer learnable coefficient, (2) spike-detector trigger, (3) depth-graded anti-correlated prior, and (4) gradient-propagation (not activation/LR) scaling is not present in any of the 8 results.
